---
title: "Quarto — Class 1: Suits Viewership"
author: "Wojciech Hardy"
date: "04/09/2023"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: false
    theme: darkly
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
```

# Introduction

*Suits* is an American legal drama television series created and written by Aaron Korsh. The series premiered on USA Network on June 23, 2011, and concluded on September 25, 2019. It follows Mike Ross, who starts working as a law associate for Harvey Specter despite never having attended law school.

![Suits Logo](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/1/13/Suits_Logo.png)

# Viewership Statistics

```{r}
#| label: data-prep
seasons <- 1:9
viewers <- c(4.28, 3.60, 2.73, 2.26, 2.01, 1.60, 1.30, 1.07, 0.96)
suits_data <- data.frame(Season = seasons, Viewers = viewers)

# Calculate season-to-season change
suits_data <- suits_data |>
  mutate(Change = Viewers - lag(Viewers))
```

```{r}
#| label: stats-table
kable(suits_data, col.names = c("Season", "Viewership (Millions)", "Change"), caption = "Suits Viewership by Season")
```

# Visualizations

```{r}
#| label: fig-viewership
#| fig-cap: "Viewership over time"
ggplot(suits_data, aes(x = Season, y = Viewers)) +
  geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "white", size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(y = "Viewers (Millions)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:9)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-changes
#| fig-cap: "Season-to-season changes in viewership"
ggplot(suits_data[-1, ], aes(x = factor(Season), y = Change)) +
  geom_col(fill = "indianred") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Season", y = "Change in Viewers (Millions)")
```
